decision_authority: author:corben-sorenson; selected publishing policy, not qualified legal advice

current_state.scope: book text, source corpus metadata, code, proofs, schemas, scripts, and assets
current_state.reuse_effect: No present license is granted without separate written permission.
current_state.opening_trigger: first author-declared completed major release after record-level rights routing

planned_completed_release_split.mixed_imported_private_local_project_tra: excluded unless separately cleared and named
planned_completed_release_split.public_metadata: remain reserved unless record-level review selects and records a compatible license

provisional_rule: Keep the active drafting repository all-rights-reserved. Do not add SPDX, Creative Commons, or Apache grant signals until the completed-release rights review resolves every included path and the exact release installs its license texts and notices.
recommendation_for_consideration: Decision implemented: delayed opening best matches the author-only drafting phase and future adoption goals. The final cleared split is CC BY 4.0 for author-owned prose/figures and Apache-2.0 for author-owned software-like artifacts; metadata and mixed material remain reserved unless separately cleared.

options.label: Retain all rights reserved
options.description: Keep the current no-reuse default and grant permissions case by case.
benefits: Maximum control over derivatives, commercial use, and source-material boundaries.
No immediate provenance classification or contribution-policy migration.
risks: Strongest adoption, collaboration, quotation, teaching, implementation, and derivative-use friction.
Public code and schemas cannot be safely reused despite being inspectable.
options.reversal_cost: Low to open later for author-owned material; prior permissions remain separately governed.

options.label: Permissive split
options.description: License author-owned prose/figures under CC BY 4.0, software-like artifacts under Apache-2.0, and author-owned public metadata under CC0 1.0, with explicit exclusions.
benefits: Maximizes legitimate reuse, translation, teaching, implementation, standards work, and citation.
Apache-2.0 supplies an explicit patent grant for software contributions.
risks: Allows commercial and modified reuse with attribution.
Irrevocable grants for released material require a careful ownership and third-party audit.
Mixed files and generated outputs need deterministic license routing.
options.reversal_cost: High for already released material because open licenses are generally irrevocable for compliant recipients.

excluded: private or local-project material without explicit permission
details.contributions: Developer Certificate of Origin or explicit inbound-equals-outbound certification

options.label: Controlled split
options.description: Use CC BY-NC 4.0 or a custom prose license, Apache-2.0 for code-like artifacts, and a separate commercial-permission path.
benefits: Enables many educational and research derivatives while reserving commercial use.
Software and schemas can still be adopted permissively.
risks: NonCommercial is ambiguous at institutional and platform boundaries and is not considered open by common open-definition standards.
Custom terms increase compatibility and enforcement complexity.
Commercial permission workflow creates ongoing administrative load.
options.reversal_cost: High for released grants and ongoing dual-permission obligations.

details.metadata: CC BY 4.0 or CC0 1.0 after audit
details.contributions: CLA likely required for relicensing flexibility

options.label: Delayed opening
options.description: Keep prose reserved during active drafting, open code/schemas now, and apply a predefined open prose license to frozen major versions after an embargo.
benefits: Allows implementation adoption immediately while preserving editorial control over the moving manuscript.
Creates a predictable path to open major-version archives.
risks: Readers may confuse latest and archived permissions.
Requires exact release manifests and license snapshots per artifact.
options.reversal_cost: Medium to high; requires durable per-version license records.

details.contributions: terms must cover both current and future release licenses

implementation_gate: The author selected delayed_opening on 2026-07-10; the active drafting repository remains all-rights-reserved.

Before the completed major release, use the deterministic path inventory to obtain author ownership assertions, inspect mixed/imported material, quarantine unresolved exceptions, and record the limits of any legal or provenance review; technical classification alone clears no path.
Keep prepublication contributions closed; define compatible inbound terms before any future contribution is accepted.
At the opening trigger, add exact CC BY 4.0 and Apache-2.0 texts, notices, deterministic file routing, exclusions, and a release-record license snapshot atomically.
Validate that rendered pages and generated artifacts carry the correct release-specific notices without licensing excluded material by implication.
non_claims: This packet is not legal advice or qualified legal review.
The selected delayed-opening policy is not a present open-license grant; LICENSE.md remains operative during drafting.
A public repository does not imply open-source or open-content rights.
